texto: Algol is the name of the winking demon star, Medusa of the skies; fair but deadly to look on, even for one who is already dying.Ah, the bright stars of the night.Almost they obliterate the clear white pain. A thousand stars shining in the ether; but no dazzling newcomer. And so little time left, so little time...Yet still two-faced Medusa laughs from behind the clouds, demanding homage. Homage, Medusa, or a sword, a blade sharper than death itself.The wind stirs. Night clouds obscure the universe. A lower music now, a different kind of death.No stars tonight, my love.No Selene.
explicacao: Citação de Elizabeth Redfern sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Algol is the name of the winking demon star, Medusa of the skies; fair but deadly to look on, eve…” articula humor, astronomy, murder em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e luto.
